name: azure-ai
description: Use for Azure AI: Search, Speech, OpenAI, Document Intelligence. Helps with search, vector/hybrid search, speech-to-text, text-to-speech, transcription, OCR. USE FOR: AI Search, query search, vector search, hybrid search, semantic search, speech-to-text, text-to-speech, transcribe, OCR, convert text to speech. DO NOT USE FOR: Function apps/Functions (use azure-functions), databases (azure-postgres/azure-kusto), general Azure resources.
capabilities: Full-text, vector, and hybrid search with Azure AI Search
Speech-to-text and text-to-speech with Azure Speech
GPT model access via Azure OpenAI
Document OCR and form extraction with Document Intelligence
Image analysis and captioning with Azure Vision
author: microsoft
securitySummary: All 195 static findings are false positives. The skill contains documentation with code examples for Azure AI SDK usage. Detected patterns (external_commands, env_access, network) are Markdown formatting artifacts and standard SDK documentation patterns, not security vulnerabilities.
valueStatement: This skill provides comprehensive documentation and SDK references for Azure AI services including Search, Speech, OpenAI, Vision, Translation, and Document Intelligence. It helps developers quickly find the right APIs and code patterns for their AI solutions.
limitations: Requires valid Azure subscription and credentials
Service-specific quotas and rate limits apply
Some features require specific Azure regions
MCP server must be enabled for tool-based access

useCases.title: Build intelligent search applications
useCases.description: Create search experiences with semantic understanding using vector embeddings and hybrid search capabilities

useCases.targetUser: Developers adding voice features to applications
useCases.title: Add speech capabilities to apps
useCases.description: Integrate real-time transcription and text-to-speech for accessibility and voice interfaces

useCases.title: Process documents at scale
useCases.description: Extract text and data from PDFs, forms, and images using OCR and form recognition

promptTemplates.title: Basic search query
promptTemplates.prompt: How do I use Azure AI Search to perform a vector search in Python?
promptTemplates.scenario: Find code examples for vector search implementation

promptTemplates.title: Speech transcription
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me how to transcribe audio files to text using Azure Speech service with Python

promptTemplates.title: Document extraction
promptTemplates.prompt: What is the code to extract text from a PDF using Azure Document Intelligence in TypeScript?

promptTemplates.title: Image analysis
promptTemplates.prompt: Give me an example of using Azure Vision Image Analysis to get captions and tags from an image URL

bestPractices: Use hybrid search combining keyword and vector queries for best relevance
Enable semantic ranking for natural language query understanding
Process documents in batches of 100-1000 for optimal performance
Use async clients for high-throughput scenarios
antiPatterns: Do not hardcode credentials - use environment variables or Azure Managed Identity
Avoid querying large datasets without pagination or batching
Do not skip error handling for API calls
Avoid using deprecated SDK versions - keep packages updated

faq.question: What is the difference between AI Search and Azure OpenAI?
faq.answer: AI Search provides search and indexing capabilities with vector support. Azure OpenAI provides access to GPT models for generative AI. They can be used together for RAG applications.

faq.question: Do I need an Azure subscription to use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes, Azure AI services require an Azure subscription. You can use free tiers for development, but production usage requires paid subscriptions.

faq.question: Can I use this skill offline?
faq.answer: No, Azure AI services are cloud-based and require network connectivity to access the APIs.

faq.question: Which programming languages are supported?
faq.answer: Azure AI services support Python, TypeScript, Java, .NET, and Go. This skill includes quick reference guides for Python, TypeScript, .NET, and Java.

faq.question: How do I enable the MCP server for Azure AI?
faq.answer: Run /azure:setup or enable via /mcp command. The MCP server provides tool-based access to AI Search and Speech services.

faq.question: What are the rate limits for Azure AI services?
faq.answer: Rate limits vary by service and pricing tier. Check Azure portal for your specific limits. Use exponential backoff for retries.
